code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent single sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # More natural template phrases – these produce only the predicate part,
    # the subject will be added once per entity.
    tmpl = {
        "league": "plays in the {o} league",
        "manager": "is managed by {o}",
        "position": "plays as a {o}",
        "ground": "home ground is {o}",
        "birthDate": "was born on {o}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {o}",
        "birthYear": "was born in {o}",
        "club": "plays for {o}",
        "draftTeam": "was drafted by {o}",
        "height": "is {o} m tall",
        "youthclub": "played youth football for {o}",
        "currentclub": "currently plays for {o}",
        "weight": "weighs {o} kg",
        "deathPlace": "died in {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "started his active years in {o}",
        "currentteam": "currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": "made his debut with {o}",
        "draftPick": "was pick number {o}",
        "draftRound": "was selected in round {o}",
        "formerTeam": "formerly played for {o}",
        "playerNumber": "wears number {o}",
        "college": "attended {o}",
        "draftYear": "was drafted in {o}",
        "coach": "is coached by {o}",
        "owner": "is owned by {o}",
        "city": "is based in {o}",
        "leader": "is led by {o}",
        "anthem": "has anthem {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {o}",
        "language": "has language {o}",
        "generalManager": "has general manager {o}",
        "chairman": "has chairman {o}",
        "season": "participated in the {o} season",
        "currency": "uses the {o} currency",
        "demonym": "people are called {o}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {o}",
        "stadium": "stadium is {o}",
        "universityTeam": "university team is {o}",
        "mostChampions": "has most championships: {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title is {o}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {o}",
        "country": "is in {o}",
        "utcOffset": "UTC offset is {o}",
        "timeZone": "time zone is {o}",
        "areaTotal": "total area is {o}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {o}",
    }

    # Group predicates by subject and predicate, collecting all objects.
    subject_predicates: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.predicate not in tmpl:
            continue
        subject_predicates.setdefault(t.subject, {}).setdefault(t.predicate, []).append(t.object)

    # Helper to build a phrase for a predicate given its objects.
    def build_phrase(pred: str, objs: list[str]) -> str:
        # Remove duplicate objects while preserving order.
        seen = set()
        uniq = [o for o in objs if not (o in seen or seen.add(o))]
        # Join objects with commas and 'and' for the last two.
        if len(uniq) == 1:
            obj_str = uniq[0]
        else:
            obj_str = ", ".join(uniq[:-1]) + " and " + uniq[-1]
        # Templates that need special handling for multiple objects.
        multi_template = {
            "club": "plays for {o}",
            "youthclub": "played youth football for {o}",
            "formerTeam": "formerly played for {o}",
            "currentclub": "currently plays for {o}",
            "currentteam": "currently plays for {o}",
            "draftTeam": "was drafted by {o}",
            "coach": "is coached by {o}",
            "manager": "is managed by {o}",
            "owner": "is owned by {o}",
            "leader": "is led by {o}",
            "ground": "home ground is {o}",
            "stadium": "stadium is {o}",
            "position": "is a {o}",
        }
        template = multi_template.get(pred, tmpl.get(pred, ""))
        if not template:
            return ""
        return template.format(o=obj_str)

    # Build sentences per subject, merging predicates.
    subject_sentences: list[str] = []
    for subj, pred_dict in subject_predicates.items():
        phrases = []
        for pred, objs in pred_dict.items():
            phrase = build_phrase(pred, objs)
            if phrase:
                phrases.append(phrase)
        if not phrases:
            continue
        # deduplicate while preserving order
        seen_ph = set()
        uniq_phrases = [p for p in phrases if not (p in seen_ph or seen_ph.add(p))]
        if len(uniq_phrases) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {uniq_phrases[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(uniq_phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + uniq_phrases[-1]
        subject_sentences.append(clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into one final sentence.
    if not subject_sentences:
        return ""
    if len(subject_sentences) == 1:
        return subject_sentences[0] + "."
    else:
        return "; ".join(subject_sentences) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence.
